target.title: Add iMessage built-in crawler
target.body:
## Intent

Add `imsgcrawl` as a first-class built-in crawler in CrawlBar. This keeps the source-native crawler ID and binary name, but presents the user-facing app name as `iMessage` in CrawlBar.

This PR is stacked on #16 so local dev binaries can be registered without replacing package-manager binaries.

## Scope

- Adds the `imsgcrawl` built-in manifest.
- Wires status, refresh/sync, search-adjacent list commands, and contact export command names from the current `imsgcrawl` command surface.
- Adds self-test coverage for the display name, command mapping, privacy metadata, native Messages icon bundle, and Messages suggestion.
- Does not add permissions UI, TCC handling, settings redesign, or package-manager install behavior.

## Proof

```sh
git diff --check
# pass

swift build
# Build complete

swift run crawlbar-selftest
# crawlbar selftest ok
```

CrawlBar manifest check:

```sh
.build/debug/crawlbarctl metadata --app imsgcrawl --json | jq '.[0] | {id, display_name, binary: .binary.name, commands: {status: .commands.status, refresh: .commands.refresh, contact_export: .commands["contact-export"]}, privacy: .privacy.contains_private_messages}'
```

```json
{
  "id": "imsgcrawl",
  "display_name": "iMessage",
  "binary": "imsgcrawl",
  "commands": {
    "status": ["--json", "status"],
    "refresh": ["--json", "sync"],
    "contact_export": ["--json", "contacts", "export"]
  },
  "privacy": true
}
```

Source contract check from the local `imsgcrawl` binary; the local binary path is omitted to avoid publishing a personal filesystem path:

```sh
<local imsgcrawl> metadata --json | jq '{id, display_name, binary: .binary.name, commands: {status: .commands.status.argv, sync: .commands.sync.argv, contact_export: .commands["contact-export"].argv}, privacy: .privacy.contains_private_messages}'
```

```json
{
  "id": "imsgcrawl",
  "display_name": "iMessage Crawl",
  "binary": "imsgcrawl",
  "commands": {
    "status": ["imsgcrawl", "--json", "status"],
    "sync": ["imsgcrawl", "--json", "sync"],
    "contact_export": ["imsgcrawl", "--json", "contacts", "export"]
  },
  "privacy": true
}
```

The shorter CrawlBar display name is intentional UI copy; the crawler ID and binary stay source-native.

## ClawSweeper Feedback

The stack-order note is handled by rebasing this PR onto the updated #16 head after #16 addressed its README docs finding. The remaining iMessage/product-scope point is intentionally maintainer review, not a contributor-side code change.

## User Prompts

Josh wanted the supported local crawlers wired into CrawlBar through small stacked PRs, starting with the CLI dev-binary path and then the iMessage crawler, without carrying the broader experimental settings/permissions redesign.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
